title: Tailscale API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don't need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Tailscale API Key detected (tskey-api- prefix). Tailscale API keys provide management access to your Tailscale network including listing and managing devices, configuring ACL policies, managing DNS settings, and administering user access. Exposing this key allows attackers to enumerate all devices on your private network, modify network access policies, add rogue devices to your VPN mesh, and potentially gain access to internal services. Revoke immediately at login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/keys.

Tailscale Auth Key detected (tskey-auth- prefix). Tailscale auth keys are used to register new devices onto your Tailscale network. Exposing an auth key allows attackers to add unauthorized devices to your private VPN mesh network, potentially gaining access to internal services and resources protected by your Tailscale ACLs. Revoke immediately at login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/keys and check for unauthorized device registrations.

Tailscale key detected (tskey- prefix). Tailscale keys (API keys, auth keys, OAuth keys, pre-auth keys) control access to your Tailscale VPN mesh network. Exposing any Tailscale key can lead to unauthorized network access, device registration, or administrative control of your private network infrastructure. Revoke immediately at login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/keys.
